revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:屈葬]]
'''Kussō''' (屈葬, Flexed burial) refers to a method of {{ill|Burial|en|Burial|qid=Q331055|lt=Burial|1=Burial}} in {{ill|Cultural anthropology|en|Cultural anthropology|qid=Q28598|lt=Cultural anthropology|1=Cultural anthropology}} and {{ill|Archaeology|en|Archaeology|qid=Q23498|lt=Archaeology|1=Archaeology}} where the deceased is positioned with bent arms and legs.<ref>https://www.oxfordreference.com/display/10.1093/oi/authority.20110803095536389</ref> It is also referred to as {{ill|Sonso|ja|蹲葬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonso|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 屈葬|1=Flexed burial|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11465624}} by some.
In China it might have been done only for those who were murdered.<ref>https://www.amazon.ca/General-History-Religions-China-Part/dp/1844642690</ref>
When the upper body is lying on its back, it is called supine flexed burial (仰臥屈葬). If the deceased is positioned on their side, it is called lateral flexed burial (横臥屈葬), and if lying face-down, it is called prone flexed burial (俯臥屈葬). A seated position is known as seated flexed burial (座位屈葬). The degree of flexion can vary: in some cases, the knees are tightly pressed against the chest, while others show a less pronounced bend, making it distinct from {{ill|Stretched burial|lt=Stretched burial|lt=Shintensō|ja|伸展葬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shintensō|qq=}}. Burial methods that incorporate bending but do not fully conform to extended burial practices blur the distinction based solely on posture.
Reasons for flexed burial practices include labor-saving during grave digging, mimicking resting postures, symbolic prayers for {{ill|rebirth rituals|ja|再生儀礼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rebirth rituals|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} through fetal positioning, or preventing the spirits of the deceased from causing harm to the living. Even the seated coffin burials practiced in Japan until recent times are considered a type of flexed burial.
== References == <references />{{Kofun navbar}}
[[File:Oguruwa shell mounds-Flexed burial.jpg|200px|thumb|Flexed burial]] {{translated page|ja|屈葬|version=92711748|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:屈葬]]
